name: Acolyte
strategyHtml: Acolytes serve as frontliners that can heal themselves or allies. Cure Touch can be used immediately when an ally drops low enough to gain the full benefit from it. Acolytes should prioritize healing heroic allies over grunt allies.
strategyText: Acolytes serve as frontliners that can heal themselves or allies. Cure Touch can be used immediately when an ally drops low enough to gain the full benefit from it. Acolytes should prioritize healing heroic allies over grunt allies.
runningMonsterHtml:
Acolytes are intended to be a base for generic grunt enemies that focus on being a healer. They can be used for any race or situation and can easily be added to fit what you need. They are the grunt version of the ‘Priest’ generic enemy.

Acolytes can function with around 10 other Grunt level allies and 1 heroic ally to pose a light threat to a party. Can be combined with other generic enemies to create a balanced composition.

Acolytes can use either a channeling weapon or a light melee weapon + shield. Acolytes can wear anything from no armor, light armor to medium armor. In the stat block, a light melee weapon + shield and light armor is assumed.

loreHtml: Acolytes are beginning spellcasters that draw their power from the gods. Due to the popularity and strength of Kari, the human goddess, the vast majority of acolyte’s in the world are human. However, acolytes of other races do still exist. Some may follow Kari, while others may follow their own god or goddess.

name: Alchemic Golem
strategyHtml:
The Alchemic Golem is a powerhouse able to take on an entire party on its own.  With its high action economy and its ability to mitigate damage every turn it can create a significant challenge for any party.  

You'll want to run the Alchemic Golem in an enclosed space where it can utilize Transmute Low Wall to force players (especially ranged ones) to waste AP on movement.  By using the wall to create a small "pool" you can then flood the pool with Alchemic Flare to prep it for next turn.  Make sure you tell players that are within the pool that they can smell gas to foreshadow the attack.  On the next turn you can use Alchemic Flare to punish any players within the pool, forcing them to abide by your mechanics.

The Alchemic Golem is a beast in close range too.  Move into melee range and deliver all of your AP in light and heavy attacks of your choice and then fire Transmutation Cannon to knock them away.  You can create a more mechanical fight by utilizing enemy positionings and walls to maximize Transmutation Cannon's damage.

Zero Impact Generator should be used anytime damage is taken by the Golem or any of its other body parts.

Alchemic Barrage is a ticking timer, that forces the party to press the attack by whittling down their RP or HP.  Once the Golem is below 50% health it unlocks Alchemic Barrage II, which allows you to focus down a player who is low RP, or force them into taking cover.

Once at half health, Alchemic Golem engages Overdrive which grants it +2 extra AP, further increasing the pressure.

You want to encourage the players to be creative and attack the specific bodyparts to reduce the Golem's action economy.  Make sure you emphasize which body part is doing which attack and that the Zero Impact Generator only protects other bodyparts.  The Golem itself has incredibly high HP, so its worth it for the party to attack those parts.  If they aren't getting the picture, try some forced skill checks to realize what is going on, or even use a NPC companion to attack the bodyparts while showcasing the effectiveness of it.

If the party attacks any body part that doesn't have a special effect, just have the damage translate to the body, but also make sure to let them know it seems to have little effect.  Once one of the bodyparts reaches 0 HP, it is destroyed and its linked actions are no longer available.

runningMonsterHtml:
Alchemic Golem is a great end of arc boss, providing a high amount of danger.  It can face a party by itself with its high action economy and can be mixed with other creatures to face off against higher level enemies.

The main body of the Golem should be at least 20ft x 20ft.

You'll also want to run this enemy in an enclosed space with a small amount of key line of sight cover.  A space around 120ft x 70ft with walls on all sides is recommended.  If you would like to do a larger space, make sure that you increase the width of Transmute Low Wall to match, but big open fields are not recommended for this enemy.

loreHtml:
Alchemic Golems are formidable constructs meticulously crafted by skilled Artificers and Alchemists. These hulking guardians come in various configurations, each tailored to serve specific security purposes within their designated domains. Typically stationed as protectors, Alchemic Golems stand sentinel over valuable treasures, ancient secrets, and critical research facilities, ensuring that only those deemed worthy may pass.

Despite their imposing size and intricate designs, each Golem exhibits a unique blend of alchemical prowess and mechanical ingenuity, reflecting the expertise of their creators. Adventurers delving into forgotten ruins or exploring cutting-edge research facilities can expect to encounter diverse variants of these sentinels. Whether guarding the remnants of a lost civilization or the forefront of arcane experimentation, Alchemic Golems present both a significant challenge and a testament to the enduring legacy of their makers.

activeActionRefs.name: Transmute Low Wall (Body)
activeActionRefs.descriptionsHtml: Creates a 3ft high wall that counts as difficult terrain.  The wall extends up to 120 feet and lasts for 10 minutes, or until Alchemic Flare or Transmute Low Wall are used, causing the wall to crumble.

activeActionRefs.name: Alchemic Flare (Body)
activeActionRefs.descriptionsHtml:
Creates an area filled with explosive gas, contained by the wall of Transmute Low Wall (Body).

If an area of gas already exists from a previous turn, ignite the gas into a fireball dealing heavy attack fire damage in the entire area that the gas was in.  Sure Hit.

name: Alpha Wolf
strategyHtml: If not engaged in melee, the alpha wolf will move up to the most vulnerable target it can reach without provoking attacks of opportunity. It will use Leading Bite against the target if it can get wolves in range with it. If an enemy is prone, it will use Go for the throat.

runningMonsterHtml:
An alpha wolf should be ran with several normal wolves to provide a good challenge for a group of adventurers. An alpha wolf cannot do much by itself and absolutely needs normal wolves around to function as an enemy.

name: Animal Summoner
strategyHtml:
The summoner spends the first action of its turn casting Summon Beast. After that, it will use Empower Beast if none of the animals are currently empowered. If there is an empowered one, it will use Bestial Command on that creature, assuming the creature is able to attack.

If the summoner is attacked, it will most likely use the animal shield ability to redirect the attack unless the summoner is high on HP/RP and the attack would kill the animal.

name: Apprentice
strategyHtml: Apprentices prefer to stay in the back, using Glittershards to block off enemy ranged attackers. After that, they will start to cast Missile Barrage until their mana runs out. If engaged in melee, will disengage and attempt to move away.

name: Archer
strategyHtml: The archer's strategy in combat is to sit still as far away as possible and do damage to the most vulnerable target, usually a spellcaster not in cover. When engaged in melee, they will swap to a melee weapon if they have one.

runningMonsterHtml:
Archers are intended to be generic grunt enemies that focus on ranged combat. They can be used for any race or situation and can easily be added to fit what you need. They are the grunt version of the ‘Sniper’ generic enemy.

Archers can function with around 10 other Grunt level allies and 1 heroic ally to pose a light threat to a party. Can be combined with other generic enemies to create a balanced composition.

Archers can use any type of ranged weapon, though pistols, shotguns and sniper rifles are rarely seen. Archers often wear no or light armor. Most will also carry a light melee weapon as a backup weapon.

If it gets targeted by an attack it will use Defensive Curl, then use Block every time it can while the stance lasts.

name: Assassin
strategyHtml: Assassins aim for the backline, targeting enemy mages or archers. They aim to creep up while stealthed, then go in for a backstab. Once they’re on a target, they will keep attacking that target, using Evasive Maneuver aggressively if needed. If they get swarmed, they will use the disengage action and retreat to wait for a better opportunity to strike again.

strongAgainst: Parties with low perception, Low defense parties
runningMonsterHtml:
Assassins are intended to be generic heroic enemies that focus on skirmishing. They can be used for any race or situation and can easily be added to fit what you need. They are the heroic version of the ‘Rogue’ generic enemy.

An assassin can be run as a threat alongside several grunts, as a small group of 2-3 assassins or in a balanced group of heroic enemies combining it with others of its type like the Defender, Sniper etc.

Assassins prefer light or no armor along with a pair of light weapons, usually daggers.

loreHtml: While the original assassins come from the desert city of Telsin, the term has evolved since then. It now refers to any sort of stealthy individual who seeks to eliminate priority targets quickly and efficiently. They exist in the employ of all major nations in the world, and people with a similar skill set are not hard to find in adventurer’s guilds, as well as other organizations.

monsterAbilityRefs.name: Flight
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionHtml:
You can Fly through the air as if you were moving on land and your movement speed is doubled while doing so.

If you take damage while flying higher than 5ft above the ground, your altitude drops by 20ft. If this would reduce your altitude to the point where you touch the ground, you become rooted and cannot fly again until the end of your next turn. If the attack that reduced your HP came from an flying creature other than yourself, you do not lose altitude.

name: Black Knight
strategyHtml:
The black knight will use Black Knight's Charge whenever it can target someone more than 20ft away. If it is surrounded by 2 or more enemies, it will use Sweep. It will also use sweep if it allows the use of the charge. After using their Black Knight's Charge, it will always use Impale if it can.

Finally, if it ever gets dismounted it will use its Esoteric Art: All Out. It makes blocking useless, but it can still dodge.

activeActionRefs.name: Black Knight's Charge
activeActionRefs.requirementsText: Must be mounted.
activeActionRefs.descriptionsHtml:
You may move up to twice your movement in a straight line towards a target further than 20ft away and gain Trample after moving at least 20ft. While under the effects of trample, you don't provoke attacks of opportunity by moving and you can move into enemies that are at least one size smaller than your mount. An enemy may spend 1 RP to move out of the way to an empty spot within 5ft of them.

If they don’t move out of the way, you make a Sure Hit Light attack against them. On damage, you knock them prone into the square you came from and keep moving, occupying the square they were in. Otherwise your movement action ends.

When you reach the target you make a Heavy attack against them. On damage, you may select a different target further than 20ft away, moving towards them and repeating this attack at no additional cost. You may only do this once.

name: Broodmother
strategyHtml:
The core part of the broodmother's strategy is the Webbing and Feast skills. The broodmother will always cast Webbing on an enemy if there are no enemies it can safely reach and attack currently inside a web. It gains Lock-on against enemies inside the web, even if they aren't rooted, so it will prioritize attacks on those enemies. It will use Poison Bite each time it can.

The feast ability has a 4 AP cost and the setup keyword, meaning that it can't immediately finish even if the broodmother has 4 AP. The smartest way to use Feast is to only use it when the broodmother has 0 AP left, this will let the broodmother get in the maximum number of attacks even if the feast attempt fails due to the target being freed from the web. However, as the GM you can use this variable cost to make the fight easier if needed.

The burrowing spiders that appear from the broodmother's passive should act on initiative 0. This gives players enough time to deal with them if they play well and they don't risk getting burrowed in without being able to do anything against it.

You prepare to eat a rooted target. When the setup finishes, you make a Heavy attack that deals maximum damage. On damage, you eat the target. The target becomes Stunned inside you. At the start of each of its turns, it takes Heavy full pierce damage until you die.

 

Any attacks made from inside you gains Sure Hit, Full Pierce and deals double damage.

activeActionRefs.name: Poison Bite
activeActionRefs.descriptionsHtml: You make a Light attack against the target. On damage, the target becomes Dazed. If the target is already dazed, it takes 8 full pierce Poison damage instead.
activeActionRefs.descriptionsText: You make a Light attack against the target. On damage, the target becomes Dazed. If the target is already dazed, it takes 8 full pierce Poison damage instead.

activeActionRefs.name: Webbing
activeActionRefs.descriptionsHtml:
You cover a 30x30ft square surface in webs. All creatures in the webbed area become slowed. The webs last until this ability is used again, or until the webs take fire damage on which they are immediately destroyed. Any creature touching the webbing when it is lit on fire becomes Burning 10.

 

Any creature that ends their turn in the webbed area becomes rooted. This lasts until they or an ally spends 4 AP to free themselves. After a unit is freed from the webs, they cannot become rooted by the same web until the end of their next turn. If the webbing is destroyed, a rooted creature is also immediately freed.

name: Brute
strategyHtml: Brute engage the frontline in combat and hit the most vulnerable target there. If they can’t make a Heavy attack, they will use Slam if possible. If they have to get in range first, they will use Charge to do so.
strategyText: Brute engage the frontline in combat and hit the most vulnerable target there. If they can’t make a Heavy attack, they will use Slam if possible. If they have to get in range first, they will use Charge to do so.
runningMonsterHtml:
Brutes are intended to be a base for generic Grunt enemies that focus on being a hardhitting melee. They can be used for any race or situation and can easily be added to fit what you need. They are the grunt version of the ‘Warrior’ generic enemy.

Brutes can function with around 10 other Grunt level allies and 1 heroic ally to pose a light threat to a party. Can be combined with other generic enemies to create a balanced composition.

Brutes generally use heavy melee weapons. They can use no armor, light armor or medium armor depending on the race. The stat block below assumes light armor and a 2handed melee weapon. Some might carry a ranged weapon as backup.

activeActionRefs.name: Charge
activeActionRefs.descriptionsHtml: You may spend 2 or 4 AP on this ability and move as though you had spent that AP on movement. After you finish moving, you may spend half of the AP you used on this ability on light or heavy melee attacks.

name: Burrowing Spider
strategyHtml: A burrowing spider will move up to the closest target. If it can, it will use Burrow. Once it is inside a host, it will use Dig Deeper each turn.
strategyText: A burrowing spider will move up to the closest target. If it can, it will use Burrow. Once it is inside a host, it will use Dig Deeper each turn.
runningMonsterHtml: A burrowing spider is not much of a threat by itself, or even in a large group. A burrowing spider is only dangerous if its ignored, since it takes them a turn of being in melee range before they use burrow and become dangerous. Have them drop down on players or come from behind or from little nooks to allow them to get close. But always give players enough time to react to them.

Any damage you take is reduced by your host’s Guard against the attack. If the host takes damage from an area attack, you are also automatically hit.

name: Crystal Behemoth
strategyHtml:
The crystal behemoth automatically reacts with Crystal Barrier when targeted with an attack if it can.

If it can use Crystal Burst, it will. Otherwise, it will use Crystal Stomp.

If it's close to an enemy, it will use Rock Crunching Bite and a mix of Light and Heavy attacks. If it struggles to reach an enemy, it will instead use Astra Beams spending all its AP on it. If it identifies a silver weapon threat, it will try to focus that enemy.
strategyText:
The crystal behemoth automatically reacts with Crystal Barrier when targeted with an attack if it can.

If it can use Crystal Burst, it will. Otherwise, it will use Crystal Stomp.

If it's close to an enemy, it will use Rock Crunching Bite and a mix of Light and Heavy attacks. If it struggles to reach an enemy, it will instead use Astra Beams spending all its AP on it. If it identifies a silver weapon threat, it will try to focus that enemy.
weakAgainst: The elemental races that absorb damage.
runningMonsterHtml:
The crystal behemoth is a suitable threat to a party. It can be defeated in two ways, either by draining its mana to 0 by letting it use crystal burst or by defeating it the normal way. The crystal burst way ensures even parties without magic users can defeat it, though they it will most likely not be a great fight for them.

Note that it will not drop a core if it kills itself with crystal burst.

Drops: Crystal Behemoth Core

name: Defender
strategyHtml: The defender’s combat strategy depends a bit on who their allies are. In general, they want to stick near their vulnerable allies and force melee enemies to attack the defender instead through Threatening Aura. If someone is Challenged, they will use Raise Shield. If they ever hit an attack of opportunity, they will always follow it up with Sweeping Opportunity.

runningMonsterHtml:
Defenders are intended to be generic heroic enemies that focus on being a tanky frontline. They can be used for any race or situation and can easily be added to fit what you need. They are the heroic version of the ‘Tank’ generic enemy.

Defenders pair best with melee attackers that don’t have as much defense as they do. They can be run with a bunch of Grunt level allies or with a group of around 3 others of a similar type to them like the Assassin or Sniper etc.

The above stat block assumes they are using Medium Armor and a Shield. Any melee weapon can work for a defender, though polearms are the best option.

name: Drake
strategyHtml:
The drake will begin the fight by flying and attacking the party from the sky. It can use both Fireball and Flame Breath in the same turn while flying. If its mana runs out before being forced to the ground, it will land itself and attack as normal.

If the drake is forced to land through its soft wings ability, it will stay grounded unless it can take flight again without provoking attacks of opportunities. It can still use its Flame Breath even while on the ground, as long as it still has mana.
strategyText:
The drake will begin the fight by flying and attacking the party from the sky. It can use both Fireball and Flame Breath in the same turn while flying. If its mana runs out before being forced to the ground, it will land itself and attack as normal.

If the drake is forced to land through its soft wings ability, it will stay grounded unless it can take flight again without provoking attacks of opportunities. It can still use its Flame Breath even while on the ground, as long as it still has mana.
runningMonsterHtml:
A group of drakes can provide a good challenge to an adventuring party, though they might be too much to handle depending on the party composition.

Running a pair of drake as a harasser from the sky can make a fight that drains the party's resources without there being a significant risk of anyone dying.

A drake is a size bigger than the wyrmling and a normal humanoid character.

Gather Parts: 250 units of Dragonscale.

monsterAbilityRefs.name: Flight

You can Fly through the air as if you were moving on land and your movement speed is doubled while doing so.

If you take damage while flying higher than 5ft above the ground, your altitude drops by 20ft. If this would reduce your altitude to the point where you touch the ground, you become rooted and cannot fly again until the end of your next turn. If the attack that reduced your HP came from an flying creature other than yourself, you do not lose altitude.

name: Dreameater
strategyHtml:
Against a single enemy, the dreameater will do a normal round of combat then do the Nightmare cage on the following round. Immediately following that with Dream Ender on the turn after.

Against a group of enemies, it will do the same thing if it wins initiative. Otherwise, it will immediately cast Nightmare Cage on the target that it was targeting while alone. Once the cage is broken, it has little else going for it and can only do basic attacks.

Should it kill a target with Dream Ender, it will keep repeating the Nightmare Cage into Dream Ender loop until all enemies are defeated.

runningMonsterHtml:
The dreameater will initially target a single player, preferably one who goes to bed early or who is separate from the rest of the party. They will wake up fighting an enemy they likely cannot defeat by themselves. Any HP or Mana they spent during this is not actually used. Afterwards, they will wake up normally and feel like they had a bad dream.

The dreameater will keep targeting the same player every night if no actions are taken.

The players must go to sleep near each other and at the same time in order to all fight it. Once they do, the dream eater will most likely be an easy kill.

If your party has less than 4 players, be sure to adjust the HP on the cage accordingly.

loreHtml: A formless creature that only ever appears in someone's dreams. It can seemingly enter the dream of anyone, no matter how far away they are. Targets people that are alone and enjoys tormenting its prey until they eventually die to exhaustion. Once it has chosen its target, it will keep visiting it until either it or the victim dies.

activeActionRefs.name: Nightmare Cage
activeActionRefs.descriptionsHtml:
You trap the target in a magical cage and teleport them next to you. The target becomes Stunned.

The cage can be targeted by attacks. Attacks against it cannot miss, it has 55 HP, no reactions and has a base guard of 0. When its HP reaches 0 or if your HP reaches 0, the cage is destroyed.

When the cage is destroyed, the trapped target may immediately take a turn after the current creature’s turn has ended, regardless of their initiative. They do double damage during this turn. Their initiative does not change based on this immediate turn.

monsterAbilityRefs.name: Just a nightmare
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionHtml: The dreameater only attacks sleeping targets and a battle against the dream eater does not take place in the physical world, but in a dreamscape created by it. A creature that is ‘killed’ by the dreameater does not die. Instead, they do not gain the normal benefits of resting and their mana is reduced by 2. If their mana would go below 0 because of this, they instead lose 5 Max HP per mana that could not be reduced. This effect lasts until the creature completes a rest undisturbed by the dreameater or until the dreameater dies.

strategyHtml:

A ranged dredge ooze will remain stationary unless it can only attack something by moving.

runningMonsterHtml:
The Dredge ooze is a creature that can snowball out of control if left unchecked. Use a lower amount of them than for a normal grunt fight, around 12 for a 4 man party. If the players have strong anti-grunt tools, you can consider adding more.

You should divide the Dredge Ooze into melee and ranged ones, and only use the ranged attack on those ranged ones. Because of their wall climb nature, you can put them on top of cliffs or hanging from the ceiling.

Gather Parts: Dredge Ooze Sludge worth 700 Clim. It's an alchemy ingredient that can be used to make [REDACTED].

name: Faewolf
strategyHtml:
The faewolf will start the battle in Shimmer. If it can, it will teleport to the squishiest looking enemy and attack it with a Heavy attack and Arcane Fang.

Every turn while it has mana, it will repeat those moves and use the remaining AP on movement or light attacks.

Every turn while it has mana, it will repeat those moves and use the remaining AP on movement or light attacks.
runningMonsterHtml:
A group of ~4 faewolves will provide a challenge to a party of 4.

Because of their nature as ambushers and their high mobility, be careful of how you run these. Three wolves teleporting in on anyone may very well down them. Likewise, three arcane fangs on the same target can add a lot of damage quickly. If running a high number of faewolves, skipping the stealth part may be best.

The wolves should all focus fire a target if you want to play them to be deadly, but otherwise you can also have them attack different targets.

Gather Parts: 500 units of Faewolf pelt

appearance: Faewolves are lean, lupine beasts whose fur shimmers like astral mist. Their eyes burn with a fae-like glow, a sign of their internal magical reserves.
habitat: They favor dense forests or snowy highlands close to fey rifts, where ambient magic saturates the environment. Lone Faewolves may venture farther if drawn by potent magical signatures—like adventurers bearing artifacts or high spirit cores.
loreHtml: Legends say these wolves were once normal forest predators who stumbled upon a fey nexus or were subject to an otherworldly curse. Over generations, they’ve adapted, developing unique teleportation powers fueled by a blend of spiritual mana and wild fae energy.

name: Frost Maiden
strategyHtml:
The Frost Maiden is an assassin that functions by limiting the enemies vision and luring them apart from each other, then striking an isolated target. 

She will start the battle by using Blizzard. On her first turn, she will use Blizzard Walker and wait around 40ft away from the enemies. If the enemies spread out to look for her, she can move in with Snow Assassin on her next turn to attack a target, then follow it up with normal attacks after.

From that point, she can alternate Blizzard Walker and Snow Assassin to get favorable engagements with a lower amount of enemies present.

From that point, she can alternate Blizzard Walker and Snow Assassin to get favorable engagements with a lower amount of enemies present.
runningMonsterHtml:
A group of frost maiden provides a challenging fight for high spirit core players.

As the GM, you can wait for a turn to see if the players will spread out and stumble into the blizzard alone to look for the frost maiden after the first turn. If they don't, then the frost maiden should just attack to not waste anybody's time. Make sure to hide the token after she moves out of vision range to further tempt people to spread out. Also, remember that if the frost maiden doesn't attack on her first turn, she can use her remaining AP to move and circle around the party to further throw them off about where she is.

monsterAbilityRefs.name: Sub-Zero Body

monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionText: Your body is freezing cold. Any melee attacks made by you deal Frost damage. Any creature touching you for an extended period of time takes Heavy Frost damage at the start of their turn.

name: Gargoyle
strategyHtml: Gargoyles will start the fight by flying over the largest group of enemies and using Stone Form. After that, they will fight normally with light and heavy attacks unless they can use Stone Form again without provoking an attack of opportunity. They may use Stone Form even when on the ground if they are isolated and about to take many hits, especially if already low on RP.
strategyText: Gargoyles will start the fight by flying over the largest group of enemies and using Stone Form. After that, they will fight normally with light and heavy attacks unless they can use Stone Form again without provoking an attack of opportunity. They may use Stone Form even when on the ground if they are isolated and about to take many hits, especially if already low on RP.
runningMonsterHtml: A small group of gargoyles of around 3-4 will provide a good threat against an average party. Gargoyles should start from a higher point, like at the top of a church or something to let them engage with Stone Form on their terms. Half Pierce attacks completely counter Stone Form, as they ignore the extra guard it gives and the gargoyle can’t react to the attacks at all while in it.

appearance: Gargoyles are medium sized creatures that at a first glance look like statues. Their skin is made of stone and they have a pair of small wings on their back.
habitat: Found in abandoned places made out of stone.
loreHtml: Gargoyles are usually tied to an abandoned place made out of stone where they will lie dormant in their stone form until people approach. When living creatures get close enough, they will reveal themselves and attack them. The reason behind their hostility remains unclear, and most adventurers that don’t want to deal with them simply avoid their resting place.

activeActionRefs.name: Stone Form
activeActionRefs.descriptionsHtml:
You turn yourself to hardened stone, gaining a +10 guard bonus but becoming unable to take any actions. All attacks made against you automatically hit, but cannot crit. You may cancel this effect at the start of your turn.

If used while flying, you drop down to the ground without taking falling damage. Any creature within 5ft of where you land may spend 1 RP or else they take Heavy damage.

monsterAbilityRefs.name: Stone Skin
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionHtml: You gain +2 guard, but lose 2 agility. (Already added to stats)
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionText: You gain +2 guard, but lose 2 agility. (Already added to stats)

monsterAbilityRefs.name: Limited Flight
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionHtml: You may fly during your movement at your normal speed, but if you are still airborne at the end of your turn, you fall to the ground taking fall damage as normal.
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionText: You may fly during your movement at your normal speed, but if you are still airborne at the end of your turn, you fall to the ground taking fall damage as normal.

name: Ghost
strategyHtml:
The ghost will start by throwing objects at its enemies. Once it is engaged in combat, it will use Spectral Touch instead of heavy attacks when attacking an armored enemy.

Against a physical attack, it can always react for free thanks to its Incorporeal ability.
strategyText:
The ghost will start by throwing objects at its enemies. Once it is engaged in combat, it will use Spectral Touch instead of heavy attacks when attacking an armored enemy.

Against a physical attack, it can always react for free thanks to its Incorporeal ability.
runningMonsterHtml: Ghost can be a pushover or almost impossible to fight depending on what the party has available. They are intended to operate in groups, but characters that rely purely on physical attacks will struggle to hit it, where as parties with casters or tools such as Sacrament will cut through the ghost without much issues.
runningMonsterText: Ghost can be a pushover or almost impossible to fight depending on what the party has available. They are intended to operate in groups, but characters that rely purely on physical attacks will struggle to hit it, where as parties with casters or tools such as Sacrament will cut through the ghost without much issues.
appearance: The ghost's appearance mimics that of the person that died, except more transparent.
habitat: Can be found in any place where a person died.
loreHtml: The ghost is an undead monster that spawned from a person that died while bearing a strong grudge. They are hostile to any living creature around them, mistaking them for the subject of their vengeance.
loreText: The ghost is an undead monster that spawned from a person that died while bearing a strong grudge. They are hostile to any living creature around them, mistaking them for the subject of their vengeance.

activeActionRefs.name: Poltergeist
activeActionRefs.descriptionsHtml: You throw an unattended object within range at a target within range, making either a Light (1 AP) or Heavy (2 AP) attack that deals normal listed damage. You do not need to physically interact with the object to throw it.
activeActionRefs.descriptionsText: You throw an unattended object within range at a target within range, making either a Light (1 AP) or Heavy (2 AP) attack that deals normal listed damage. You do not need to physically interact with the object to throw it.

activeActionRefs.name: Spectral Touch
activeActionRefs.descriptionsHtml: You attempt to attack the soul of the target with a touch, making a Heavy attack against the target.
activeActionRefs.descriptionsText: You attempt to attack the soul of the target with a touch, making a Heavy attack against the target.

monsterAbilityRefs.name: Incorporeal
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionHtml: You do not spend your RP when reacting against a physical attack made with a non-magical weapon. A weapon that is enchanted counts as magical.
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionText: You do not spend your RP when reacting against a physical attack made with a non-magical weapon. A weapon that is enchanted counts as magical.

monsterAbilityRefs.name: Phase through
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionHtml: You may move through solid objects and enemies with no penalty.
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionText: You may move through solid objects and enemies with no penalty.

name: Ghoul
strategyHtml:
If a ghoul can eat a corpse with Devour Corpse, it will always do so. It may even move to the corpse to do it if it can do so safely.

When in combat, it will use its Ghoul Bite if it can and it is missing HP. Otherwise, it will attack normally.
strategyText:
If a ghoul can eat a corpse with Devour Corpse, it will always do so. It may even move to the corpse to do it if it can do so safely.

When in combat, it will use its Ghoul Bite if it can and it is missing HP. Otherwise, it will attack normally.
runningMonsterHtml: A large group of ghouls can make a good challenge for a group of adventurers. Ghouls become a lot more powerful if they fight in a room or place that has a lot of corpses ready to be devoured. Ghouls can also use Devour Corpse on the bodies of other ghouls.
runningMonsterText: A large group of ghouls can make a good challenge for a group of adventurers. Ghouls become a lot more powerful if they fight in a room or place that has a lot of corpses ready to be devoured. Ghouls can also use Devour Corpse on the bodies of other ghouls.
appearance: A humanoid creature with grey skin, sharp teeth and red eyes.
habitat: Usually found in places with lots of dead bodies.
loreHtml: Ghouls are undead that are perpetually hungry. They feed on the corpses of dead creatures, preferably humanoids.
loreText: Ghouls are undead that are perpetually hungry. They feed on the corpses of dead creatures, preferably humanoids.

activeActionRefs.name: Ghoul Bite
activeActionRefs.descriptionsHtml: You attempt to bite a target creature making a Heavy attack against them. You heal for the damage this attack deals.
activeActionRefs.descriptionsText: You attempt to bite a target creature making a Heavy attack against them. You heal for the damage this attack deals.

activeActionRefs.name: Devour Corpse
activeActionRefs.descriptionsHtml: You eat a nearby corpse, instantly devouring it and removing it from the field. You gain +2 AP and your maximum AP is increased by 2 until the end of the encounter.
activeActionRefs.descriptionsText: You eat a nearby corpse, instantly devouring it and removing it from the field. You gain +2 AP and your maximum AP is increased by 2 until the end of the encounter.

monsterAbilityRefs.name: Holy Vulnerability
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionHtml: Holy damage dealt to you is treated as Full Pierce damage.
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionText: Holy damage dealt to you is treated as Full Pierce damage.

monsterAbilityRefs.name: Revel in Death
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionHtml: If there's a corpse within 5ft of you at the start of your turn, you heal for half your maximum HP and gain a +4 bonus to accuracy checks.
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionText: If there's a corpse within 5ft of you at the start of your turn, you heal for half your maximum HP and gain a +4 bonus to accuracy checks.

name: Giant Astra Crab
strategyHtml: It's very adaptive, e.g. it will try to counter its opponent's actions. However, it doesn't plan ahead, it simply reacts. It just wants food, and will try to escape death. Peaceful resolution may be possible with sufficiently large offerings?

One giant crab is a good challenge for a group of 4 SC3000+ characters.

Gather Parts: DC25 to harvest a Perfect Shell.

The ultimate lifeform.

activeActionRefs.name: Adapt
activeActionRefs.descriptionsHtml: You may shift up to 2 stat points (except Toughness). The minimum is 3 and maximum is 9.
activeActionRefs.descriptionsText: You may shift up to 2 stat points (except Toughness). The minimum is 3 and maximum is 9.

activeActionRefs.name: Crab Flexibility
activeActionRefs.descriptionsHtml: You Block or Dodge an attack. Then, you automatically Dodge or Block (whichever you didn't before) against the next attack.
activeActionRefs.descriptionsText: You Block or Dodge an attack. Then, you automatically Dodge or Block (whichever you didn't before) against the next attack.

activeActionRefs.name: Perfect Shell
activeActionRefs.descriptionsHtml: You Block a ranged attack, as long as the accuracy roll is below your Dodge value.
activeActionRefs.descriptionsText: You Block a ranged attack, as long as the accuracy roll is below your Dodge value.

activeActionRefs.name: Ultimate Lifeform
activeActionRefs.descriptionsHtml: You remove all stacks of one status effect from yourself.
activeActionRefs.descriptionsText: You remove all stacks of one status effect from yourself.

activeActionRefs.name: Crab Grab
activeActionRefs.descriptionsHtml:
You make a Light attack against the target. On damage, the target becomes rooted until it takes at least 10 damage in one attack. Medium and smaller creatures are forced to move with the crab, but large creatures grabbed by this prevent you from moving more than 5ft away.

Cannot be used against a target already grabbed.

You may let go of a target for 0 AP. You suffer the root penalty for dodging against the target (but only against the target).

monsterAbilityRefs.name: Hard Shell
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionHtml: You have +1 guard and +4 Block, but -2 Evasion (already added to stats). You also have 5 DR against slashing attacks.
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionText: You have +1 guard and +4 Block, but -2 Evasion (already added to stats). You also have 5 DR against slashing attacks.

monsterAbilityRefs.name: Skitter
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionHtml: Your movement in a straight line gains +10 movement speed.
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionText: Your movement in a straight line gains +10 movement speed.

name: Giant Mantis
strategyHtml:
At the start of the battle, it will leaping strike to the most vulnerable looking target it can safely reach. It will not provoke attacks of opportunity to do so.

Once it’s engaged in combat, it will keep attacking the most vulnerable looking target. It will use slice and dice whenever it can.
strategyText:
At the start of the battle, it will leaping strike to the most vulnerable looking target it can safely reach. It will not provoke attacks of opportunity to do so.

Once it’s engaged in combat, it will keep attacking the most vulnerable looking target. It will use slice and dice whenever it can.
runningMonsterHtml:
The Giant Mantis can cut through unarmored targets extremely quickly if it gets a surprise round. If the party does not have high perception values, then it may be best to not run more than 2 at the same time. If running more than 2, it may be better to forego the stealth part.

Note that the Giant Mantis poses very little threat to an armored target, but it's danger level rises if the party has no armored people.

activeActionRefs.name: Leaping Strike
activeActionRefs.descriptionsHtml: You jump to a target within range and make a Light attack against it.
activeActionRefs.descriptionsText: You jump to a target within range and make a Light attack against it.

activeActionRefs.requirementsHtml: You must have dealt damage to the target this turn.

monsterAbilityRefs.name: Stalk
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionHtml: You are camouflaged as you stalks your prey. You gain a bonus +10 to stealth checks and can move at full speed while hiding. When you get a surprise round, your initiative is treated as if you had rolled the maximum possible.
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionText: You are camouflaged as you stalks your prey. You gain a bonus +10 to stealth checks and can move at full speed while hiding. When you get a surprise round, your initiative is treated as if you had rolled the maximum possible.

monsterAbilityRefs.name: Razorsharp
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionHtml: You bladelike arms are razor sharp, causing the target to bleed when hit. When you deal damage to a target wearing no armor or light armor, you deals an additional Power true damage.
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionText: You bladelike arms are razor sharp, causing the target to bleed when hit. When you deal damage to a target wearing no armor or light armor, you deals an additional Power true damage.

name: Giant Prismatic Slime
strategyHtml: If the slime is out of range to attack, it will use Bowling Roller. If it is in range to use Gluttony, it will do so. It will use Digestion when it can on the turn after it hits with Gluttony.
strategyText: If the slime is out of range to attack, it will use Bowling Roller. If it is in range to use Gluttony, it will do so. It will use Digestion when it can on the turn after it hits with Gluttony.
runningMonsterHtml: A solo monster against which regular attacks will do little. A group with heavy magic damage may be able to kill it normally, but dealing damage without landing a critical hit is nearly impossible. Make sure to give the players enough hints and guidance so that they can realize this.

habitat: Can be found anywhere with astra influence.
loreHtml: A fiend that appears like a giant slime. Seeks to devour the mana of other creatures.
loreText: A fiend that appears like a giant slime. Seeks to devour the mana of other creatures.

activeActionRefs.name: Bowling Roller
activeActionRefs.descriptionsHtml: You begin to quickly roll towards a target with little control.  You move in a straight line towards the target, immediately stopping at the first target or large object in your path and making a Heavy attack against that target.
activeActionRefs.descriptionsText: You begin to quickly roll towards a target with little control.  You move in a straight line towards the target, immediately stopping at the first target or large object in your path and making a Heavy attack against that target.

activeActionRefs.name: Digestion
activeActionRefs.requirementsText: Affected by Gluttony.
activeActionRefs.descriptionsHtml: You attempt to digest all targets affected by Gluttony. Dealing 20 (type) damage with sure hit and full pierce.
activeActionRefs.descriptionsText: You attempt to digest all targets affected by Gluttony. Dealing 20 (type) damage with sure hit and full pierce.

activeActionRefs.name: Gluttony
activeActionRefs.descriptionsHtml: You attempt to consume a target, making a Heavy Attack against the target.  On hit, the target is affected by Gluttony and becomes engulfed in slime.  An engulfed target becomes Weakened.  An ally can attempt to pull a consumed target from the slime with a DC20 Power Check, removing the effects of Gluttony.
activeActionRefs.descriptionsText: You attempt to consume a target, making a Heavy Attack against the target.  On hit, the target is affected by Gluttony and becomes engulfed in slime. monsterAbilityRefs.name: Core Brain
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionHtml: Critical attacks deal 125 bonus true damage to you.
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionText: Critical attacks deal 125 bonus true damage to you.

monsterAbilityRefs.name: Gel Body
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionHtml: You cannot use basic defensive actions: Block or Dodge, but you have 100 Damage Reduction to all non-magical attacks.
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionText: You cannot use basic defensive actions: Block or Dodge, but you have 100 Damage Reduction to all non-magical attacks.

monsterAbilityRefs.name: Insatiable Hunger
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionHtml: You can use Gluttony as an attack of opportunity for 1 RP.
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionText: You can use Gluttony as an attack of opportunity for 1 RP.

monsterAbilityRefs.name: Prismatic Adaptability
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionHtml: When struck by a magical attack, you gain 100 Damage Reduction to that attack element indefinitely. You can only have one instance of this adaptability at a time.
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionText: When struck by a magical attack, you gain 100 Damage Reduction to that attack element indefinitely. You can only have one instance of this adaptability at a time.

name: Giant Spider
strategyHtml: The Giant Spider will attempt to Web Spray the closest target if it is not in melee range. Afterwards, it will move up and attack using poison bite whenever it can.
strategyText: The Giant Spider will attempt to Web Spray the closest target if it is not in melee range. Afterwards, it will move up and attack using poison bite whenever it can.
runningMonsterHtml: A large group of giant spiders can provide a decent challenge to a group of adventurers. Should be combined with other grunt spiders to create a more varied encounter.
runningMonsterText: A large group of giant spiders can provide a decent challenge to a group of adventurers. Should be combined with other grunt spiders to create a more varied encounter.
appearance: A large spider.
habitat: The giant spider is most commonly found underground or in caves where a broodmother has set up her lair.
loreHtml: A large spider that preys on regular people.
loreText: A large spider that preys on regular people.

activeActionRefs.name: Poison Bite
activeActionRefs.descriptionsHtml: You make a Light attack against the target. On damage, the target becomes Dazed. If the target is already dazed, it takes 8 full pierce Poison damage instead.
activeActionRefs.descriptionsText: You make a Light attack against the target. On damage, the target becomes Dazed. If the target is already dazed, it takes 8 full pierce Poison damage instead.

activeActionRefs.name: Web Spray
activeActionRefs.descriptionsHtml:
You make a Light attack against the target that deals no damage, but covers the target in webs. On hit, the target becomes rooted.This effect lasts until the target or an ally spends 4 AP to remove the webbing.

 

If a creature rooted by this ability takes fire damage, the effect also disappears as the webs burn away.
activeActionRefs.descriptionsText:
You make a Light attack against the target that deals no damage, but covers the target in webs. On hit, the target becomes rooted.This effect lasts until the target or an ally spends 4 AP to remove the webbing.

If a creature rooted by this ability takes fire damage, the effect also disappears as the webs burn away.

monsterAbilityRefs.name: Spider Climb
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionHtml: You can walk on walls as if it were solid ground. You ignore webbing and other difficult terrain created by spiders.
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionText: You can walk on walls as if it were solid ground. You ignore webbing and other difficult terrain created by spiders.

name: Gravity Fiend
strategyHtml:
The gravity fiend starts the combat by using Exousiai's Presence. On each of its turns it will use Weight of the World, followed by Gravity Crash if possible.

After that, it will use Gravity Pull to get enemies in range then use Crushing Presence if it can hit enough enemies.

It has high regeneration and is intelligent. If it is threatened, it can attempt to create distance to give it a turn to heal.
strategyText:
The gravity fiend starts the combat by using Exousiai's Presence. On each of its turns it will use Weight of the World, followed by Gravity Crash if possible.

After that, it will use Gravity Pull to get enemies in range then use Crushing Presence if it can hit enough enemies.

It has high regeneration and is intelligent. If it is threatened, it can attempt to create distance to give it a turn to heal.
runningMonsterHtml:
The Gravity Fiend is not something you should use against a party and expect them to beat it without preparation. Against an unprepared party of people that are most likely running max burden, it can easily inflict heavy damage or kill people without much counterplay. Due to this, it is rated as danger level EX.

It functions as a solo monster against a whole party of 5 even when they do prepare. Against an average prepared party, this can be considered to be a D rated threat.

activeActionRefs.descriptionsHtml:
You raise the astra level in the zone by 1. All enemies within range that do not have enough resistance to negate this immediately suffer negative effects.

Any creatures suffering from Astra Poisoning immediately have their Astra Poisoning worsened by 1 stage.

activeActionRefs.descriptionsHtml:
Every enemy in range suffers +1 Burden until the end of the encounter. This can stack.

You immediately kill all grunts and summons.

activeActionRefs.descriptionsText: You make a Heavy attack against all enemies within range.

activeActionRefs.name: Gravity Pull
activeActionRefs.descriptionsHtml: You pull every enemy within range up to 40ft.
activeActionRefs.descriptionsText: You pull every enemy within range up to 40ft.

monsterAbilityRefs.name: Superboss Resistance

monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionText: You are immune to any status effects that would cause you to lose AP or otherwise prevent you from acting.

monsterAbilityRefs.name: Gravity Well
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionHtml: All enemies within 40ft of you are slowed. This applies immediately when they enter this range and lasts for the entire movement of leaving this range.
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionText: All enemies within 40ft of you are slowed. This applies immediately when they enter this range and lasts for the entire movement of leaving this range.

monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionHtml: Any attack against you from further than 40ft away automatically misses.
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionText: Any attack against you from further than 40ft away automatically misses.

monsterAbilityRefs.name: Hover
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionHtml: You can passively hover up to 5ft above the ground. While hovering, you are not an eligible target for abilities that require a target to be flying or airborne unless you'd meet those conditions normally.
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionText: You can passively hover up to 5ft above the ground. While hovering, you are not an eligible target for abilities that require a target to be flying or airborne unless you'd meet those conditions normally.

name: Greater Astran Magpie
strategyHtml: A greater astran magpie follows the targeting described in its Strike for the Shiny, smartly! ability. They use Swooping Strike II almost exclusively, unless they can attack without moving already.
strategyText: A greater astran magpie follows the targeting described in its Strike for the Shiny, smartly! ability. They use Swooping Strike II almost exclusively, unless they can attack without moving already.
runningMonsterHtml:
1 or 2 Greater Astran Magpie along with a large group of normal Astran Magpie's makes a decent challenge for a group of adventurers. While a greater astran magpie can be harder to hit than other enemies, keep in mind its damage out is comparatively lower than other heroic enemies.

Gather Parts: 250u of feathersteel from their talons, 250u of direhide from the pelt.

monsterAbilityRefs.name: High Flock Tactics
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionHtml: You gain a +1 bonus to your accuracy rolls (max +6) for each other Astran Magpie attacking the same target this round.

monsterAbilityRefs.name: Strike for the Shiny, Smartly!
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionHtml: You are predisposed to attack targets who are displaying the most valuable items, Crafter numerical bonuses (Focus, Power, Crafting skill) are not perceptible but things like layering for armor or alloying weapons are, You prioritize value on weapons and artifacts, but if none of that is visible then you will default to attacking the one with the shiniest armor. You also consider current health, attempting to down (not finish off, just down) low health >25% HP targets as top priority, it’s easier to loot people who can’t fight back after all!

monsterAbilityRefs.name: Flight
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionHtml:
You can Fly through the air as if you were moving on land and your movement speed is doubled while doing so.

If you take damage while flying higher than 5ft above the ground, your altitude drops by 20ft. If this would reduce your altitude to the point where you touch the ground, you become rooted and cannot fly again until the end of your next turn. If the attack that reduced your HP came from an flying creature other than yourself, you do not lose altitude.

name: Greater Manticore
strategyHtml:
The greater manticore has 2 separate attack phases.

The first attack phase should be using the head to attack, starting with Lion's Roar and then attacking slowed targets to gain the bonuses from the crushing maw ability.

The second attack phase should use the tail, using Tail Spikes and then attacking targets that were hit by it if possible.

runningMonsterHtml:
The greater manticore is a solo monster that has a lot more AP than a normal creature would have thanks to its dual headed ability. You don't need to stick to the head and tail attack phases, but it will make it easier to run and for players to understand.

Gather Parts: 1000 units of primehide.

habitat: Can be found anywhere in the wild or in a dungeon.
loreHtml: The greater manticore is a monstrous combination of three different types of animal originally fused together through magic. They possess little intelligence and do little more than feast on anyone unfortunate enough to run into one. Their second head is a mutation caused by astra corruption that only makes it more vicious.

monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionHtml: You take two turns. Regardless of initiative, your first turn always comes first in a round. Your second turn comes after half of your enemies have taken their turn.

name: Gryphon
strategyHtml:
The gryphon will stay in the sky and harrass with Wind Blast until its mana runs out. If it lands a hit and knocks a target prone, it will follow up with Dive Attack on the target.

If the enemy has a lot of ranged attackers or if they are too spread out for a Wind Blast, the gryphon might go in earlier with a Dive Attack and use regular attacks instead.

You move up to 40ft towards a target within range and then make a Light attack against it. On damage, you do not provoke attacks of opportunity for the rest of your turn and may move up to your movement speed at the end of your turn without spending AP.

 

If this attack fails to do damage,you lose your Flight ability and become Grounded until the start of its next turn.

name: Heliamphora
strategyHtml:
The opening for a Heliamphora is to wait for a target to walk in range of its vines without noticing the plant. It may use Luring Pollen to do so.

After its surprise attack, it will try to put any grabbed creatures in its pitcher whenever possible. If any creatures are more than 10ft away from it, it will use its pollen to lure them closer. After that, it will try to grab as many creatures as possible using its basic attack and vine grab.

runningMonsterHtml:
Make sure the players are aware of the threat of the pitcher once a player has been grabbed by describing the creature as attempting to move the player to its pitcher. Being dropped in it takes a player out of the fight and may get them killed.

Some players may also need help in recognizing that they can destroy the vines. If you want to go easy on the players, you can delay the first drop in pot and drop hints that the vines can be targeted.

strategyHtml: The boar rider will charge towards the enemy backline, aiming to trample as many enemies on the way there as possible.

activeActionRefs.name: Boar Trample
activeActionRefs.requirementsText: Must be mounted.
activeActionRefs.descriptionsHtml:
You may move up to three times your movement in a straight line and gain Trample for this movement. While under the effects of trample, you don't provoke attacks of opportunity by moving and can move into enemies that are at least one size smaller than your mount. An enemy may spend 1 RP to move out of the way to an empty spot within 5ft of them.

If they don’t move out of the way, you make a Sure Hit Light attack against the,. On damage, you knock them prone into the square you came from and keep moving, occupying the square they were in. Otherwise your movement action ends.

Cannot be used when an enemy unit is visible within 10 feet of you.

strategyHtml:
A hobgoblin fighter hits the closest target it can reach. If there are multiple, it will hit the one with the least armor. If it cannot reach any target, it will throw a rock.

When a hobgoblin goes below half HP, it will use Gobbo Regeneration.

name: Hydra
strategyHtml: The hydra will open with their Hydra Breath attacks if the enemy isn’t in range, or if they can hit 3+ people with it. Otherwise, they will attempt a constrict into Three Headed Assault on a target when possible.
strategyText: The hydra will open with their Hydra Breath attacks if the enemy isn’t in range, or if they can hit 3+ people with it. Otherwise, they will attempt a constrict into Three Headed Assault on a target when possible.
runningMonsterHtml:
The Hydra can serve as a solo monster for a party of 4. The regenerative nature of the heads can make it feel to the players that they are not making progress or doing the wrong thing, so make sure to emphasize that the hydra is being slowed and weakened when the heads grow.

The Hydra lacks a strong central body for the players to target, so you can just put 3-5 head tokens to represent the heads (it dies after getting 6+ heads). The heads shouldn’t move more than 5ft away from each other.

appearance: The hydra is a creature with three heads resting on a long neck attached to a rather small frame. It is usually found with its body below the water with only the three heads sticking out. The necks and heads are covered in colored scales with each of the heads having a different color that marks their element. The Earth one is green, the Frost one is white and the Lightning one is golden.
habitat: Almost always found near water. Due to the size of the hydra, usually a large river or lake.
loreHtml: A weird animal that has three heads that each have their own thoughts. Each head itself is an intelligent creature (though they lack the ability to speak a common language), but the heads themselves are always in conflict. They struggle to make a decision on even the most basic things, sometimes resorting to infighting. Only when their existence is threatened by an outside source do the heads work together.

On damage, you apply an effect based on the head’s element. The Earth head inflicts Daze for 1 turn, the Frost head inflicts Slowed for 1 turn and the Lightning head inflicts Static X for 1 turn, where X is your Power x 3.

monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionHtml: Each head that grows extra past the original 3 lowers your agility by 1 and movement speed by 5ft. When your agility reaches 0 because of this, you die.

name: Ice Queen
strategyHtml:
The ice queen will start by summoning a tower near her enemies with both her encounter start action and her normal ability, then immediately follow it up with Reverbant Frost.

She wants to keep away from melee enemies as much as possible. If she can escape them without provoking, she will do so. Future towers will be placed to either slow melee enemies on top of her, or in the biggest group of enemies. She will always follow up a tower placement with Reverbant Frost.

If she has the option to hit slowed enemies safely with Shards of Ice, she will do so. Otherwise she will attack with whatever weapon is most suitable for the situation. She can easily use a ranged weapon to safely attack more vulnerable opponents.
strategyText:
The ice queen will start by summoning a tower near her enemies with both her encounter start action and her normal ability, then immediately follow it up with Reverbant Frost.

She wants to keep away from melee enemies as much as possible. If she can escape them without provoking, she will do so. Future towers will be placed to either slow melee enemies on top of her, or in the biggest group of enemies. She will always follow up a tower placement with Reverbant Frost.

If she has the option to hit slowed enemies safely with Shards of Ice, she will do so. Otherwise she will attack with whatever weapon is most suitable for the situation. She can easily use a ranged weapon to safely attack more vulnerable opponents.
runningMonsterHtml:
The ice queen is a solo monster that is both intelligent and cunning. She may avoid direct fights if they don't appear to be in her favor or attempt to negotiate if things look dire.

Most of her threat comes from the towers. If players are dedicated to instantly destroying them, she may need reinforcements to keep up the threat.

appearance: A beautiful woman with pale or blue skin and white hair. Stands out from the average yuki-onna or frost maiden with her regal stature and the crown of ice she wears on her head.
habitat: Can be found in any place where it freezes for most of the year.
loreHtml:
An ice elemental with high intelligence that commands other elementals and beasts in the area. She does this without directly communicating with them, merely her presence in the area is enough for ice creatures to submit.

She is domineering, but can be reasoned and negotiated with if shown the proper respect.

activeActionRefs.name: Frost Creation: Tower
activeActionRefs.descriptionsHtml:
You summon a 10ft high frosty tower within range. The tower has 45 HP, 4 guard and no reactions. Attacks against the tower always hit.

The tower acts at initiative 0, at the end of a round. When it does, it makes a single light attack using the your stats at every enemy within 20ft of it. This attack has half pierce, deals Frost damage and on damage, the enemy is Slowed until the end of their next turn.

activeActionRefs.name: Queen's Domain
activeActionRefs.descriptionsHtml:
You shape the area around you to be like that of your hive. The area within 20ft of you gains the following properties:

  • You gain Tremorsense in your hive area, becoming aware of the exact location of all creatures touching the ground.
  • Any non-flying creature except for you, a devouring swarm or a summoner contracted to you is slowed in the area. 
  • When an enemy dies while in the domain, you may choose to spawn an Ixith swarm in that location without paying the normal cost as a swarm bursts from the corpse. These do not count against your normal limits. In addition, when an enemy dies in your domain you heal for your Toughness x2

Upkeep: 1 Mana. When you pay upkeep, you may increase the radius of your hive by 10ft.

monsterAbilityRefs.name: Flight
monsterAbilityRefs.descriptionHtml:
You can Fly through the air as if you were moving on land and your movement speed is doubled while doing so.

If you take damage while flying higher than 5ft above the ground, your altitude drops by 20ft. If this would reduce your altitude to the point where you touch the ground, you become rooted and cannot fly again until the end of your next turn. If the attack that reduced your HP came from an flying creature other than yourself, you do not lose altitude.

name: Landshark
strategyHtml:
Landsharks are extremely hard to hit while they are not attacking. They become vulnerable when they leap out to bite, allowing for readied attacks or other reaction attacks to hit them easily. 

Because ranged attackers cannot easily do this, they become the easiest target to focus on for Landsharks. If given the option, Landsharks should always aim to attack ranged attackers over melee ones. Landsharks do not provoke opportunity attacks, allowing them to swim past the melee line. Thus if they can reach a ranged attacker and attack them they should do so. Otherwise, they will attack the melee attacker closest to them if already in range and then swim away.

name: Mage
strategyHtml:
Mages prefer to stay in the back, using either Glittershards to block off enemy ranged attackers or using Arcane Net to entrap enemy melee attackers. Possibly both. Afterwards, they will use Missile Barrage to soften up all enemies. Depending on their allies and the battle situation, mages may want to retain some mana to recast the net or glittershards. 

If they are under threat, they will want to reserve 1 mana to make Block a valid option. A mage engaged in melee combat can’t do much, they will have to disengage and move away.

runningMonsterHtml:
Mages are intended to be a base for generic heroic enemies that focus on being both a ranged controller and AoE striker. They can be used for any race or situation and can easily be added to fit what you need. They are the heroic version of the ‘Apprentice’ generic enemy.

Mages can function with Grunt level allies, though they will need to be set up in situations where their control abilities can shine. They can function in a group with around 3 generic heroic level enemies like the Defender, Assassin etc.

Mages generally use a channeling weapon and wear no armor.

activeActionRefs.name: Goring Charge
activeActionRefs.descriptionsHtml:
You lower your head as you charge and look to crash into either the target or an obstacle barring your path. Any object that is not made of sufficiently thick metal or stone (DM Discretion) is destroyed in the charge. Any creature behind said object is immediately knocked Prone and takes Light damage.

If this attack hits a creature, you will make a Heavy attack on the target. When using more than 2 AP on this movement, you gain an additional 1 focus and 2 power on this attack. On damage if the creature has less toughness than you, they are immediately knocked Prone and Dazed.

name: Priest
strategyHtml:
A priest will want to play close to the frontlines to be in range for Angel’s Embrace. Once the melee’s of each side have engaged on combat, the priest can drop a hallowed ground in such a way that it affects all of their melee allies. Afterwards, they will focus on attacking and will use their remaining mana to cast Cure Touch once an ally (heroic allies only if there is any left) becomes low on HP.

Angel’s Embrace can be used to absorb big hits on allies, though the priest should stop using it unless to prevent a killing blow once their own RP becomes too low.

runningMonsterHtml:
Priests are intended to be a base for generic heroic enemies that focus on being both a support and healer. They can be used for any race or situation and can easily be added to fit what you need. They are the heroic version of the ‘Acolyte’ generic enemy.

Priests can function with Grunt level allies, though their support will not be nearly as effective as when supporting heroic allies. They can function in a group with around 3 generic heroic level enemies like the Defender, Assassin etc.

Priests can use either a channeling weapon or a light melee weapon + shield. Priests can wear anything from no armor, light armor to medium armor. In the stat block above, a light melee weapon + shield and light armor is assumed.

loreHtml: Priests are spellcasters that draw their power from the gods. Due to the popularity and strength of Kari, the human goddess, the vast majority of priests in the world are human. However, priests of other races do still exist. Some may follow Kari, while others may follow their own god or goddess.

name: Raiju
strategyHtml:
The Raiju's strategy is the same regardless of whether its storming or not. It will start by using Static Field at encounter start.

On its turn, it will use Static Charge to pass through as many enemies as it can, without having to move before using Lightning Charge. The remaining AP will be used on attacking.

On its turn, it will use Static Charge to pass through as many enemies as it can, without having to move before using Lightning Charge. The remaining AP will be used on attacking.
runningMonsterHtml:
A large group of Raiju will provide a decent challenge for a group of adventurers. During a storm, you'll want to lower the amount if you want to keep the difficulty similar as the same group of Raiju can quickly murder a group of low level adventurers.

If it is hunting one of the party members, you can have the Raiju focus on that party member even at risk to themselves.

Gather Parts: 250 units of animal hide.

appearance: A white wolf like creature around which electricity constantly crackles.
habitat: Rarely found anywhere in the wild. Only seen by those it is hunting.
loreHtml:
The raiju is often confused for an animal due to its appearance. In reality, its an elemental that serves as the hunting dog for a more powerful elemental creature. It will track and hound an enemy from afar, then move in for the kill once the weather changes.

Storms have an unnaturally high chance of appearing when this creature is around.

name: Skeletal Monstrosity
strategyHtml:
The Skeletal Monstrosity’s battle strategy is simple. It will attack a random target (or the target that did the most damage to it the previous round) and use Skeletal Armblade Barrage. Afterwards, it will use the sweep against the largest group of targets. After those two attacks, it will keep making light and heavy attacks as appropriate.

If there are no enemies in his attack range, which is 10ft, it will use Bone Spike Shot until an enemy is pulled within its range, then continue with the above mentioned strategy.

If there are no enemies in his attack range, which is 10ft, it will use Bone Spike Shot until an enemy is pulled within its range, then continue with the above mentioned strategy.
runningMonsterHtml:
The Skeletal Monstrosity is a good enemy to face by itself. Against stronger parties, you can add a few normal skeletons to the mix or give the creature additional arms to further boost its action economy.

Token wise, you want to have a bigger token for the main body (10x10ft token) and then 4 5x5ft tokens for each of the arms. The arms should be adjacent to the main body in some way.

Each arm has 40 HP and uses the Dodge and Guard of the main body. They have 1 RP, which regenerates at the start of your turn. Each arm is attached to the main body and moves along with it. They otherwise have a movement speed of 0 and are immune to Knockbacks, Prone and other disabling effects. An AoE attack only does damage to 1 part of your body, either the main body or an arm, which is chosen by the attacker.

name: Sniper
strategyHtml:
The sniper’s strategy in combat is to sit still as far away as possible and do damage to the most vulnerable target. What the most vulnerable target is may change during the course of the battle and a sniper can easily adapt to the situation.

They can use Homing Shot when dealing with high dodge characters. If they are engaged in melee combat, they will use Disengaging Shot if they have mana for it. If they don’t, the common strategy is to switch to a melee weapon.

runningMonsterHtml:
Snipers are intended to be generic heroic enemies that focus on ranged combat. They can be used for any race or situation and can easily be added to fit what you need. They are the heroic version of the ‘Archer’ generic enemy.

Snipers can be run together with several grunt level enemies or in a group together with around 3 similar heroic enemies like the Defender, Assassin etc.

Snipers can use any type of ranged weapon, though pistols, shotguns and sniper rifles are rarely seen. Snipers often wear no or light armor. Most will also carry a melee weapon as a backup weapon.

name: Tactician
notableSkills: Knowledge History+11, Perception+10, Negotiation+11, Intimidation+11, Deception+10
strategyHtml: The tactician relies mostly on their troops to do the damage. Their job is to apply Focus Fire to the target most vulnerable or which is reachable by the most of their troops. They can use reposition to cut off escapes or make their allies easily reach the backline. They will always use Stratagem: Lightning at the start of the encounter.
strategyText: The tactician relies mostly on their troops to do the damage. Their job is to apply Focus Fire to the target most vulnerable or which is reachable by the most of their troops. They can use reposition to cut off escapes or make their allies easily reach the backline. They will always use Stratagem: Lightning at the start of the encounter.
runningMonsterHtml:
Tacticians are intended to be generic heroic enemies that focus on tactics and leadership. They can easily be used for any race or situation that requires it.

A tactician is worthless without a good number of grunt level allies. They can pair well with both melee attackers with Stratagem: Lightning and Reposition, but also ranged attackers due to easier Focus Fire usage.

With Battle Tactics, you are free to play the enemies as smartly as you can, roleplaying it as the tactician giving precise orders. Tacticians can use a wide variety of weapons and armor depending on the race or society they are from. Ranged weapons are generally preferable, as a tactician going down may lead to an instant loss.

name: Tank
strategyHtml: The tank’s strategy is to move up to take space, then provoke the closest melee enemy he can.
strategyText: The tank’s strategy is to move up to take space, then provoke the closest melee enemy he can.
runningMonsterHtml:
Tanks are intended to be generic grunt enemies that focus on being a tanky frontline. They can be used for any race or situation and can easily be added to fit what you need. They are the heroic version of the ‘Defender’ generic enemy.

Tank can function with around 10 other Grunt level allies and 1 heroic ally to pose a light threat to a party. Can be combined with other generic enemies to create a balanced composition.

The above stat block assumes they are using Medium Armor and a Shield. Any melee weapon can work for a tank

name: Unstable Efreet
strategyHtml: Unstable Efreet’s strategy is very simple. They will run towards the nearest enemy and attack them until they die and explode.
strategyText: Unstable Efreet’s strategy is very simple. They will run towards the nearest enemy and attack them until they die and explode.
runningMonsterHtml: For the most effective use, Unstable Efreets should not start too far away from the players, otherwise they might cause a chain reaction of explosions before they get in range. Be careful when putting these against a Pyromancer. Pyromancers can trivialize this encounter by tagging them with fire spells, but if they do it while the Unstable Efreets are too close, then they could cause a chain reaction that leads to a constant barrage of explosions on the players.

loreHtml: Unstable Efreets are split off parts of a normal Efreet. Their very existence is a fleeting thing with them being able to exist for little more than day before their flames going out and them vanishing into nothingness.

name: Warrior
strategyHtml: Warriors use Charge to engage the frontline in combat and hit the most vulnerable target there. If engaged with two or more enemies, they will use Cleaving Strike. Else, they will use Skullsplitter if the target is wearing at least medium armor.
strategyText: Warriors use Charge to engage the frontline in combat and hit the most vulnerable target there. If engaged with two or more enemies, they will use Cleaving Strike. Else, they will use Skullsplitter if the target is wearing at least medium armor.
runningMonsterHtml:
Warriors are intended to be a base for generic heroic enemies that focus on being a hardhitting melee. They can be used for any race or situation and can easily be added to fit what you need. They are the heroic version of the ‘Brute’ generic enemy.

Warriors can function with Grunt level allies, but they will want some tank type allies to not get focused down as hard. They can function in a group with around 3 generic heroic level enemies like the Defender, Assassin etc.

Warriors generally use heavy melee weapons. They can use no armor, light armor or medium armor depending on the race. The stat block above assumes light armor and a heavy melee weapon. Some might carry a ranged weapon as backup.

name: Westrian Heavy Paladin
strategyHtml: Westrian Heavy Paladins aim to charge towards the enemy backline with mounted charge, aiming to move through as many enemies as possible to get there. Once they are in melee range, they will use Divine Smite on each of their turns while their mana lasts. As Divine Shield is free, they will use it at the start of each turn.

Once they are in melee range, they will use Divine Smite on each of their turns while their mana lasts. As Divine Shield is free, they will use it at the start of each turn.
runningMonsterHtml:
A small group of around 4 heavy paladins would make a challenging encounter for a group of players. As they are mounted enemies, they benefit from an open fighting field and are unsuitable for indoor fights.

Heavy Paladins are intelligent creatures and veteran fighters. Don't be afraid to play them intelligently. If a paladin can hit a demon in their divine release state with their divine smite, they should always do so unless it would cause them to provoke too many attacks.

activeActionRefs.name: Mounted Charge
activeActionRefs.requirementsText: Must be mounted.
activeActionRefs.descriptionsHtml:
You may move up to twice your movement in a straight line towards a target further than 20ft away and gain Trample after moving at least 20ft. While under the effects of trample, you don't provoke attacks of opportunity by moving and you can move into enemies that are at least one size smaller than your mount. An enemy may spend 1 RP to move out of the way to an empty spot within 5ft of them.

If they don’t move out of the way, you make a Sure Hit Light attack against them. On damage, you knock them prone into the square you came from and keep moving, occupying the square they were in. Otherwise your movement action ends.

When you reach the target you make a Heavy attack against them
