name: Wight
strategyHtml: The wight will attacks the closest target. It can choose between Spectral Touch or Draining Touch + a light attack, both are valid options.
strategyText: The wight will attacks the closest target. It can choose between Spectral Touch or Draining Touch + a light attack, both are valid options.
runningMonsterHtml:
Wights are very strong against physical attackers. Even a small group of wights can be annoying to fight when you don't have any non-physical damage. Consider the party's abilities very carefully when choosing how many wights to put in.

You can choose to have Wights use their RP immediately, which opens them up for physical attackers, or have them save their RP so they can always react to the physical attackers. The first option will make the fight more manageable for the party members who don't have magical damage options.

strategyHtml: The wolves will try to swarm a target, though they won't take attacks of opportunity to do so. They will keep using topple prey until the target is prone, then the other wolves will use go for the throat.

name: Wraith
strategyHtml:
The Wraith will avoid bright light the best it can, only going in if it has no other choice. It may use Darkness in this case to negate the effects of it if possible.

If it doesn't need to avoid bright light, it will use Darkness after spending 3 AP, then immediately follow up with Darkness Shade to hide.

For attacks, it will use the Spectral Touch and Draining Touch. Like other incorporeal enemies, it can freely react to any physical attacks without burning RP.

For attacks, it will use the Spectral Touch and Draining Touch. Like other incorporeal enemies, it can freely react to any physical attacks without burning RP.
runningMonsterHtml:
A group of wraith's can provide a good challenge to a high spirit core party. At this point, most people should have a way of dealing non-physical weapon. The wraith's stats are a bit higher than the other incorporeal enemies, so be careful because some members of the party may have 0 chance of hitting it even with that.

Having a source of bright light also helps the players. A torch alone wouldn't qualify, but a northi lantern or a paladin's holy weapon might.

appearance: A translucent dark creature with a vaguely humanoid shape.
habitat: Found only in permanently dark places.
loreHtml: A vengeful spirit that died in the darkness while it was still alive. Brought back to life through a necromancer or through astra corruption, they attack any living creature that they can see. They are weak to bright lights and will never willingly go into the sunlight.

activeActionRefs.name: Darkness
activeActionRefs.descriptionsHtml:
You conjure an area of pure darkness in a 30x30 ft area around it, centered on yourself. In the darkness, non-magical light ceases to function.

 

The darkness lasts until your next turn.

activeActionRefs.requirementsText: In a dark area.
activeActionRefs.descriptionsHtml: If you are in a dark area, you attempt to Hide without using AP. When hiding in darkness, you may stay hidden if you are in direct Line of Sight of others. Leaving the dark area or having any source of light illuminate the area will immediately reveal you.

name: Wyrm (Fire)
strategyHtml:
The firewyrm will rain fire from the sky until it is forced down to the ground or it runs out of mana. Even when out of mana, it may still choose to do fly-by attacks instead of staying on the ground.

While in the sky, it can use both Fireball and Flame Breath at the largest group of enemies. On the ground, it will use tail swipe as the final action of each round and may still use flame breath if it has mana left.

At the encounter start, it will always use Terrifying Roar.

runningMonsterHtml:
The firewyrm is a solo monster that is a serious threat to any party without aeromancers. In a space where their flight is limited, they can be less threatening. As they are intelligent creatures, a firewyrm may retreat if the battle starts going badly for it.

The firewyrm is bigger than a drake, but the token size is still the same.

Gather Parts: 1000 units of dragonscale.

Though they spent most of their time sleeping in their caves, they are hostile to all living creatures and view them are little more than prey. Wyrms are older, more intelligent and more dangerous than their Drake and Wyrmling counterparts.

name: Zombies
strategyHtml: Zombies move towards the near enemy. If the target is not already grabbed (by any zombie) and they can make a Heavy attack, they make a Heavy attack. Otherwise if they are not already grabbing an enemy, they will do so.

runningMonsterHtml: A large group of zombies is a decent challenge for a group of adventurers. Zombies are weak to holy damage, so you will want to add more of them to keep the challenge the same when the players have classes like a paladin or priest with them. Zombies are slow, but relatively tanky for a grunt enemy. Against parties that are less likely to exploit this weakness or if fighting in a place where its impossible to do, you may want to use slightly less zombies.

activeActionRefs.name: Grab
activeActionRefs.descriptionsHtml:
You make a Light attack against the target. On damage, you and the target are both Rooted.

The target may spend 1 AP to break free, after which the target and you are both no longer rooted. If the target is grabbed by multiple enemies through this ability, spending the 1 AP will free it from all grabs.
